title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect at midnight on April 16, 2026, pausing hostilities with Hezbollah after six weeks of intense cross-border strikes that began March 2 amid broader Iran tensions. Hezbollah, not formally party to the agreement, launched rockets hours before implementation, underscoring the truce's fragility despite a reported calm allowing Lebanese civilians to return home. Israel retains troops in southern Lebanon and self-defense rights, while President Trump announced an upcoming White House summit between Netanyahu and the Lebanese president to pursue a permanent deal. Ongoing diplomatic efforts and potential violations remain key risks to extension beyond the initial period.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain leads Polymarket's 2026 FIFA World Cup winner market at 17.2% implied probability, just ahead of France at 16.2%, with England (10.9%), Argentina (8.8%), and Brazil (8.6%) clustered tightly behind, reflecting trader consensus on a fiercely competitive 48-team field post-qualification playoffs. Spain's edge stems from their dominant UEFA qualifying campaign—topping their group unbeaten—and sustained momentum from the Euro 2024 triumph, bolstered by golden generation talents like Lamine Yamal and Pedri. France closed the gap after impressive March international window results, showcasing depth with Kylian Mbappé at peak form and a balanced squad. The bunched odds highlight the expanded format's upset risks from dark horses like Haaland-led Norway (2.3%) and semifinalists Morocco (1.8%), alongside South American volatility and neutral-site group stage dynamics across North American venues.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's league-best 64-18 regular season record, clinched April 12 for the second straight year with elite net rating above 11—historically a championship marker—and full home-court advantage propel their 45.5% implied probability as trader consensus favorite entering playoffs Saturday. San Antonio Spurs at 14.8% reflect Victor Wembanyama's blocks-leading 25.0 PPG/11.5 RPG dominance and recent Magic Johnson Award, plus their season series edge over OKC as No. 2 West seed. Boston Celtics' 12.2% stems from veteran core and No. 2 East position despite defensive lapses; Denver Nuggets' 9.5% rides Nikola Jokić's double-double threat amid a 10-game win streak. Detroit Pistons' youth caps their 4.7% at East No. 1, highlighting playoff inexperience risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom holds trader consensus at 27.5% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by his executive experience, national fundraising network, and high-profile criticism of Trump administration policies during a February book tour and amid a March California poll showing him leading Kamala Harris by 14 points in their home state. Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trails at 8.5% on progressive enthusiasm, while Harris sits at 6.8% despite recent April signals of interest in another run, reflecting party reassessment after her 2024 general election loss. Sen. Jon Ossoff's 6.6% rise stems from youth and Georgia battleground appeal. In this wide-open field ahead of 2026 midterms, consolidation could hinge on midterm results, early primary polling in Iowa and New Hampshire, key endorsements, and fundraising tallies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim lead in trader consensus for the 2028 presidential election at 19%, with California Governor Gavin Newsom close behind at 17%, driven by early positioning as Republican successor to term-limited President Trump and the top Democratic contender amid party rebuilding. Recent CPAC straw poll results placed Vance atop the GOP field, while a March UMass Lowell matchup showed him edging Newsom 33%-30%, underscoring tight dynamics in fragmented primaries. Rising odds for Marco Rubio and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez reflect VP visibility, gubernatorial records, and progressive appeal, but low volume on others signals uncertainty. The 2026 midterms, Trump endorsements, scandals, or announcements could widen gaps in this closely contested early market.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran negotiations toward a permanent peace deal, mediated by Pakistan, hinge on resolving core disputes over nuclear curbs, missile limits, sanctions relief, and regional proxy activities amid a fragile two-week ceasefire that began April 7 after US strikes degraded Iranian military infrastructure. President Trump stated April 17 that no sticking points remain and an agreement is very close, following stalled 21-hour talks in Islamabad on April 12, marking the first direct US-Iran discussions. A parallel 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire bolsters de-escalation, but the US pushes for a 20-year uranium enrichment ban while Iran resists perpetual limits. Traders watch the April 21 ceasefire deadline for escalation risks or breakthroughs in ongoing diplomacy.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

markets.question: Will there be no change in Fed interest rates after the April 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 99.3% implied probability of no Federal Reserve rate change at the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus around the Fed funds target range of 3.50%-3.75% amid persistent inflation pressures and labor market resilience. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since mid-2024—driven by energy costs from geopolitical tensions, while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs, edging unemployment to 4.3%. March FOMC minutes highlighted elevated inflation risks and openness to hikes among some officials, aligning with CME FedWatch hold odds near 98%. A realistic challenge would require softer-than-expected pre-meeting data like retail sales or April producer prices to revive cut expectations, though upside inflation surprises could marginally boost hike odds.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published for the Active Month WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on WTI crude oil prices for April 2026 reflects a sharp pullback after Middle East de-escalation, with Iran confirming the Strait of Hormuz fully open, erasing geopolitical risk premiums and driving front-month futures down over 10% last week to near five-week lows around $84–$91 per barrel as of April 16 close at $94.69. U.S. crude inventories unexpectedly drew by 913,000 barrels to 463.8 million for the week ended April 10, about 1% above five-year averages, amid steady refinery runs. Bearish pressures stem from non-OPEC+ supply growth forecasts and softening global demand signals, with Goldman Sachs eyeing a $78 average for 2026. Key catalysts ahead include the April 23 EIA inventory report and OPEC's monthly outlook on May 13, which could recalibrate supply expectations.

title: Republican Presidential Nominee 2028
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, edging Vice President J.D. Vance at 39% and Senator Marco Rubio at 21%, capturing a fragmented early field following Donald Trump's 2024 victory and ongoing shadow primary. RFK Jr.'s lead stems from his Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement's momentum, highlighted by a POLITICO poll last week showing his criticism eroding corporate America's image among voters, plus his cousin's March confirmation of a definite 2028 bid leveraging health policy challenges to big agriculture and pharma lobbies. Vance retains frontrunner status in CPAC and Yale Youth straw polls due to incumbency and Trump endorsements, while Rubio gains from Senate visibility. The tight contest persists absent clear separation; 2026 midterms, potential Trump backing shifts, or primary announcements could tip balances in swing states or among key voting blocs like MAHA supporters.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 62.5% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic returning to normal by April 30, reflecting persistent shipper caution amid a U.S. naval blockade on Iranian ports that began April 13. Despite Iran's April 17 announcement declaring the strait "completely open" for commercial vessels and President Trump's report of sea mine removal, transits remain at roughly 5% of pre-crisis levels, with tanker volumes 90% below normal due to elevated war-risk insurance premiums and security uncertainties. Over 34,000 routes have diverted since early April disruptions, underscoring the chokepoint's role in 20% of global oil flows. Key catalysts include ceasefire progress, formal security guarantees, and vessel tracker data through month-end.

title: Counter-Strike: Vitality vs Natus Vincere (BO3) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 2 match between Vitality and Natus Vincere in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 17 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality win the match against Natus Vincere.

This market will resolve to "Natus Vincere" if Natus Vincere win the match against Vitality.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 2 match between Vitality and Natus Vincere in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 17 at 2:15PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Vitality and Natus Vincere play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality, the world #1, meets #2 Natus Vincere in a high-stakes IEM Rio 2026 playoffs quarterfinal BO3, with the winner advancing to face FURIA in the semifinals. Vitality secured a Group A runner-up spot via a gritty 2-1 win over G2 but suffered a 1-2 upset loss to Falcons two days ago, ending their match win streak and exposing minor vulnerabilities. NaVi clawed through Group B's lower bracket, capping a resilient run with a 2-1 victory over Aurora. Vitality dominates recent head-to-heads 16-2, including a 3-0 BLAST Rotterdam grand final three weeks ago, though NaVi's map strengths on Mirage and Nuke could force a competitive veto. Stable rosters highlight ZywOo's firepower against w0nderful's rifling, amid CS2's evolving meta favoring aggressive T-sides.

title: Counter-Strike: Spirit vs MOUZ (BO3) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Quarterfinal 1 match between Spirit and MOUZ in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 17 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against MOUZ.

This market will resolve to "MOUZ" if MOUZ win the match against Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit holds the edge in trader consensus for their IEM Rio 2026 playoffs quarterfinal BO3 against MOUZ, driven by a commanding head-to-head record (22-15 overall, including recent 2-0 sweeps) and superior three-month HLTV stats (1.38 rating, 0.90 KPR vs. MOUZ's 1.14 and 0.74). Spirit advanced through groups despite shaky form—clutching against lower seeds like RED Canids amid donk's flu complaints—but boast firepower from tN1R (2.15 recent rating) and sh1ro. MOUZ (#8 HLTV) upset Legacy and Aurora but lost 0-2 to FURIA days ago, struggling on T-side against tier-1 teams with hally sidelined. Map vetoes favor Spirit's Dust2 strength over MOUZ's Mirage pick, where execution and clutches decide advancement.

title: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for April 17 2026 in Indian Premier League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders - Who wins the toss?
markets.description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for 2026-04-17 in Indian Premier League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Indian Premier League: Gujarat Titans vs Kolkata Knight Riders - Completed match?
markets.description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between Gujarat Titans and Kolkata Knight Riders scheduled for 2026-04-17 in Indian Premier League is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gujarat Titans hold momentum heading into their IPL home clash against winless Kolkata Knight Riders at Narendra Modi Stadium, fresh off back-to-back victories that lifted them to sixth on the points table with two wins from four games. KKR, struggling through a free-falling start without a single triumph, face further setbacks with Matheesha Pathirana unavailable due to travel logistics, forcing lineup tweaks like Tim Seifert's inclusion over Finn Allen. Ahmedabad's batting-friendly belter pitch favors high-scoring T20 encounters, where GT's lethal bowling attack—led by Kagiso Rabada, Mohammed Siraj, Prasidh Krishna, and Rashid Khan—could exploit KKR's top-order flops and spin reliance on Sunil Narine and Varun Chakaravarthy. Hot conditions with minimal dew and no major injury disruptions for GT underscore their edge in form and depth.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli command trader consensus in the F1 Drivers' Championship market, mirroring their 1-2 positions in early 2026 driver standings—Antonelli atop with 72 points from a perfect start including back-to-back China Grand Prix triumphs, ahead of Russell's 63. Recent developments like Antonelli's 100% win rate from pole and race pace dominance in Mercedes' superior machinery under new regulations have propelled his 30.2% implied probability, but traders favor Russell at 40.5% for his qualifying benchmark, consistency, and experience mitigating rookie pressures. Distant challengers like Leclerc (6.2%) lag amid Red Bull reliability woes and McLaren's qualifying deficits, with Miami Grand Prix pivotal for the intra-constructor title fight.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sassuolo's 2-1 victory over fifth-placed Como at Mapei Stadium earlier today has driven trader consensus to a dominant 100% on the home win in this Serie A matchup, aligning with the confirmed result amid final resolution formalities. Despite Como's strong campaign—58 points from 32 games, unbeaten away in 2026 across competitions, and the league's best defense conceding just 26 goals—Sassuolo capitalized on solid home form, securing three wins in their prior four at the venue while overcoming suspensions to Josh Doig and Domenico Berardi plus multiple defender injuries. Como, with minimal absences beyond Jayden Addai, had previously swept the season head-to-head but faltered here. Realistic challenges include rare VAR overturns or official protests altering the scoreline.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's "Liekinheitin" by Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen commands a dominant 36.9% implied probability as the clear frontrunner, fueled by its explosive blend of classical violin and pop energy that won Finland's UMK national final on February 28 and has sustained massive fan buzz through orchestral versions and stage previews, topping bookmakers and OGAE fan votes since. France's Monroe with the chic "Regarde!" (11.8%) benefits from Big 5 pre-qualification and strong vocal appeal post its March 6 reveal, while Denmark's emotive ballad "Før vi går hjem" by Søren Torpegaard Lund (10.4%) surged on recent live performances after its DMGP victory. Australia's Delta Goodrem "Eclipse" holds steady at 7.2% on star power. With Vienna semis looming May 12-14, trader consensus reflects early momentum amid ongoing rehearsals, though jury-televote splits and late entries could shift dynamics.

title: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei rejected President Trump's April 17 claim that Tehran agreed to hand over its enriched uranium stockpile, insisting the material is "as sacred as Iranian soil" and immovable, amid ongoing 2025–2026 US-Iran nuclear negotiations. Sticking points include US demands for full export or downblending under IAEA monitoring versus Iran's resistance, with a proposed $20 billion unfreezing of Tehran's frozen assets as incentive and unresolved gaps on enrichment bans (20 years sought by Washington, 5 offered by Tehran). IAEA's February report noted 440kg of 60% enriched uranium stored underground at Isfahan with access denials. Second-round talks are slated for Islamabad this weekend, as prior special forces seizure discussions underscore high military risks before May 31 resolution.

title: Hornets vs. Magic
description:

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Eastern Conference NBA Play-In Tournament's decisive No. 8 seed matchup at Kia Center, surging Charlotte Hornets (43-38) face host Orlando Magic (45-36) with playoff hopes on the line. Hornets enter red-hot off a gritty play-in win over Miami, dominating the season series 3-1 with a 20.3-point average margin and 45.7% three-point shooting against Orlando. Center Moussa Diabate is questionable with left hip soreness, their lone notable injury concern, while Magic boast fuller health but recent dysfunction after a play-in loss to Philadelphia. LaMelo Ball's availability bolsters Charlotte's backcourt edge, though Orlando's home-court advantage and defensive prowess could fuel an upset in this elimination thriller.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile two-week ceasefire between the United States, Israel, and Iran—mediated by Pakistan—took effect on April 8, 2026, halting direct military exchanges after six weeks of intense US-Israeli strikes on Iranian nuclear, missile, and naval targets, alongside Iranian retaliations and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. On April 15, the parties agreed in principle to extend the truce beyond April 22 amid Islamabad talks, while a separate 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire began April 16 and Iran reopened the Hormuz strait for shipping. President Trump expressed optimism for a permanent end, but reports of minor violations underscore risks of escalation before the deadline, shaping trader consensus on de-escalation timelines.

title: 2026 Men's French Open Winner
description:
The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The 2026 French Open is scheduled for May 18 - June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 French Open Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after July 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the French Open (https://www.rolandgarros.com/en-us/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's commanding straight-sets victory over Carlos Alcaraz in the 2026 Monte Carlo Masters final has propelled trader consensus to price him at 44.5% implied probability for the French Open title, affirming his world No. 1 ranking and rapidly improving clay-court prowess after opening the swing with dominant wins. Alcaraz, the defending Roland Garros champion with exceptional surface history, holds steady at 34.5% despite the final loss and recent withdrawal from Barcelona, underscoring a closely contested duel between the top two. Alexander Zverev's consistent clay form earns 4.0%, while Novak Djokovic languishes at 2.9% amid ongoing injury concerns and age-related doubts despite his three prior titles. With Madrid looming, focus shifts to rest advantages and draw luck on the slow Paris clay.

title: Peru Presidential Election Winner
description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who wins the next Peruvian Presidential election. 

This market includes any potential second round. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori's commanding first-round performance in Peru's April 12-13 presidential election drives trader consensus favoring her as the overall winner, with 93% of ballots counted by ONPE showing her at 17% amid a fragmented field of over 20 candidates. Roberto Sánchez Palomino holds a slim second-place lead at 12% over Rafael López Aliaga's 11.9%, by roughly 8,000-14,000 votes bolstered by rural tallies, positioning a likely June 7 runoff matchup. Fujimori's edge stems from pre-election polling dominance, focus on crime and corruption amid ongoing instability under interim President Boluarte, and potential right-wing consolidation, though López Aliaga's fraud allegations and pending urban/expatriate votes add uncertainty to the runoff pairing.

title: UEFA Champions League Winner
description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League (soccer).

markets.question: Will Real Madrid win the 2025–26 Champions League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed team will win the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team is officially crowned the winner of the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to win the tournament (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 UEFA Champions League is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA Champions League (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern Munich holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the UEFA Champions League title after a dramatic 4-3 second-leg comeback victory over Real Madrid, securing a 6-4 aggregate quarterfinal win that boosted their momentum heading into the semifinal against defending champions PSG, who dominated Liverpool 4-0 on aggregate. Arsenal advanced past Sporting CP with a gritty 3-0 aggregate (1-0 first leg, 2-0 second), setting up a tactical clash with resilient Atletico Madrid, who edged Barcelona 3-2 overall despite a 2-1 second-leg loss. The bunched probabilities among the final four reflect evenly matched knockout pairings, strong recent form across domestic leagues, and no major injury disruptions, keeping the race wide open ahead of first legs on April 28/29.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US airstrikes targeted military installations on Kharg Island, Iran's critical Persian Gulf oil export hub handling nearly 90% of its crude shipments, most recently on April 7 amid escalating tensions with the US and Israel. Earlier raids on March 13 and threats from President Trump to seize the facility have disrupted operations but not dislodged Iranian control, as satellite imagery through April 12 shows sustained export activity and tankers departing as late as April 14. No ground invasion or verified handover has occurred, despite IRGC provocations prompting the strikes. Traders monitor potential further military actions, diplomatic negotiations, or Iranian retaliation as pivotal factors that could shift control dynamics before market resolution dates.

title: Brazil Presidential Election
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including CNT/MDA on April 14 showing President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 44.9% versus Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 40.2% in a simulated runoff, and Nexus/BTG Pactual on April 15 indicating a 46-46 tie, underscore the razor-thin trader consensus for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election first round, with Lula at 39.5% and Flávio at 38.2%. Lula's approval has dipped to 44% amid economic headwinds and high disapproval, enabling Flávio—Jair Bolsonaro's son and right-wing frontrunner—to consolidate conservative support in a fragmented field. The race remains deadlocked due to polarized voter bases and steady polling averages; catalysts like economic rebounds, scandals, key endorsements, or Lula's health could tip the balance toward a decisive second-round matchup.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Mets (7-12), reeling from Juan Soto's placement on the 10-day injured list with a right calf strain last week, face the Chicago Cubs (9-9) at Wrigley Field in the opener of a three-game series. Kodai Senga (0-2, 7.07 ERA) starts for the road-struggling Mets against Edward Cabrera (1-0) for Chicago, highlighting a pitching mismatch amid New York's road losing streak and additional injury concerns like Jorge Polanco (questionable Achilles). Cubs' Seiya Suzuki rides a three-game hitting streak post-injured list return, bolstering home-field momentum in potentially windy April conditions at the Friendly Confines.

title: Porsche Tennis Grand Prix: Iga Swiatek vs Mirra Andreeva
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Iga Swiatek and Mirra Andreeva in the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix, originally scheduled for April 17, 2026 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Iga Swiatek' if Iga Swiatek advances against Mirra Andreeva.

This market will resolve to 'Mirra Andreeva' if Mirra Andreeva advances against Iga Swiatek.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: No. 3 seed Iga Swiatek faces surging No. 6 Mirra Andreeva in the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix quarterfinals on indoor clay at Porsche Arena, marking their first clay-court clash after three hard-court meetings. Swiatek, a two-time Stuttgart champion with unmatched clay pedigree, enters amid a shaky 2026 (around 12-6 record) featuring early WTA 1000 exits and adjustments under new coach Tomas Roig, though she advanced comfortably in prior rounds. Andreeva boasts momentum from her Linz WTA 500 clay title last week, extending a six-match streak with straight-sets wins over Jelena Ostapenko and Alycia Parks here; she's undefeated on clay this year. Head-to-head favors the teenager 2-1, fueling trader interest in an upset amid Swiatek's top-10 vulnerabilities.

title: Bitcoin above ___ on April 18?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

title: Next French Presidential Election
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls following March 2026 municipal elections position Jordan Bardella of the National Rally and Édouard Philippe of Horizons as top first-round contenders for France's April 2027 presidential election, with Bardella leading at 34-38% and Philippe at 18-25%, yet Philippe narrowly prevailing 51.5-48.5% in Elabe's head-to-head runoff. Philippe's reelection as Le Havre mayor boosted his centrist appeal amid fragmented center-right and left-wing fields, where Jean-Luc Mélenchon trails at 12-15%, tempering RN momentum despite its perceived strengthening. Trader consensus underscores this tight race, driven by widespread desire for post-Macron change. Party nominations, potential left-wing primaries, or security/economic shifts could create separation ahead of the first round.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 14 - April 21, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 14 12:00 PM ET to April 21, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 14 12:00 PM ET to April 21, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket centers around 260-299 total posts from Elon Musk over April 14-21, with 260-279 edging out at 22.5% implied probability and 280-299 close behind at 21%, reflecting his steady pace of 30-40 posts per day tracked in recent weeks—including 270 for April 10-17 (averaging 39 daily) and 30 already by April 17 amid AI policy debates, WhatsApp privacy scrutiny, and Grok advancements. This tight race underscores competitive dynamics driven by Elon's variable social media rhythm, spiking during viral moments like political commentary or X platform news, versus quieter periods focused on Tesla or SpaceX. Key differentiators include potential catalysts like upcoming earnings reports or election-cycle buzz through April 21, with historical highs near 100 daily posts possible but tempered by recent consistency; monitor daily trackers for momentum shifts as the week progresses.
